context: Tests whether the agent implements cohort retention SQL using monthly truncation with the correct milestone periods, builds a behavioral rules engine with all three rule types and correct operator semantics, uses parallel rule evaluation, and recommends the correct index strategy.

checklist.name: DATE_TRUNC for cohort month
checklist.description: cohort_analysis.sql uses DATE_TRUNC('month', ...) to define the cohort month (not EXTRACT, not date casting alone)

checklist.name: Four retention milestones
checklist.description: The SQL tracks retention at exactly periods 1, 3, 6, and 12 months (all four must be present as separate output columns)

checklist.name: Exclude invalid orders
checklist.description: The cohort SQL excludes orders with status 'cancelled' AND 'refunded' (both statuses must be filtered out)

checklist.name: Event rule type
checklist.description: The TypeScript segment rule type includes an 'event' variant with at least an event name, a count comparison (op + value), and a time window in days

checklist.name: Property rule type
checklist.description: The TypeScript segment rule type includes a 'property' variant with a field name, comparison operator, and value

checklist.name: Segment membership rule type
checklist.description: The TypeScript segment rule type includes a 'segment' variant that checks whether a customer is (or is not) in another segment

checklist.name: AND/OR operator support
checklist.description: The BehavioralSegment type includes an 'operator' field accepting both 'AND' and 'OR' values

checklist.name: Promise.all parallel evaluation
checklist.description: evaluateBehavioralSegment uses Promise.all (not sequential awaits) to evaluate all rules concurrently

checklist.name: Correct operator semantics
checklist.description: The AND operator uses .every(Boolean) and the OR operator uses .some(Boolean) on the results array

checklist.name: Composite index recommendation
checklist.description: db_recommendations.md recommends a composite index on the customer_events table covering customer_id, event, and created_at (all three columns)

checklist.name: Pre-materialization recommendation
checklist.description: db_recommendations.md recommends pre-materializing segment membership (e.g. in a nightly job or materialized view) as a performance strategy

checklist.name: AGE() for period calculation
checklist.description: cohort_analysis.sql uses AGE() or DATE_PART('month', AGE(...)) to compute the period number (months since cohort), not a simple date subtraction
